rulings.Case Title: TESHOME T TAREKEGN, VS. FREWWORK ABERRA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider And For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF TESHOME TAREKEGN's Motion for Reconsideration and for Leave to Amend Second Amended Complaint DENIED. Plaintiff fails to sustain burden under CCP 1008(b). Additionally, even if the Court construes this motion as a CCP 473(b) motion, it is also DENIED as no proposed pleading was submitted with the motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MONICA D. KENNEY VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend as follows: Second cause of action for fraud - to allege a false statement regarding June 28, 2011 foreclosure and to allege facts showing Plaintiff's actions in reliance on June 27, 2011 preventing Plaintiff from challenging/addressing June 28, 2011 foreclosure. Third cause of action for negligence - to allege misrepresentation as to the status of modification as of June 27, 211 and/or status of modification as to "the date, time, or status of a foreclosure sale." Lueras v. BAC Home Loans (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 49. Fourth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation -to allege a false statement regarding June 28, 2011 foreclosure; and to allege to allege misrepresentation as to the status of modification as of June 27, 211 and/or status of modification as to "the date, time, or status of a foreclosure sale." Fifth cause of action for wrongful foreclosure - to allege all elements of this cause of action and to allege tender; or to allege a cause of action fro UCL violation. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CHUNG VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., FEDERAL HOME LOAN MORTGAGE CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULLED in part. COAs 2, 8, & 11 are SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege facts showing the loan agreement was a loan modification that would create a duty of care under Alvarez v. BAC Home Loans Servicing, Docket No. A138443. COA 7, for fraud by Freddie Mac, is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to plead facts, not conclusory statements, proving detrimental reliance, as well as to plead the authority of the various individuals to speak on behalf of the corporation. COA 13, for forcible eviction, is OVERRULED. Complaint states sufficient facts showing threats and physical acts by Freddie Mac to constitute a claim. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILLIAMS VS. MICHAEL BRENDSEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, line 11. DEFENDANT MICHAEL BRENDSEL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT overruled 5 days to answer. Defendant states no authority for the proposition that notice of tenant's rights to re-occupy the premises is excess of what is statutorily required renders the Notice of Termination fatally defective. Defendant's demurrer is not base don the misjoinder of parties under CCP 430.10(d) and no other grounds for standing to raise the issue are asserted. Omitted claimants have a right to file a claim of right to possession. Good faith and ulterior motive is an issue of fact not properly addressed on demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUES LANDY VS. ROBERT PIGGOTT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANT ROBERT PIGGOTT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT overruled, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges unlawful detainer action. Whether habitual late payment is a curable or a non-curable violation is a factual issue. Expiration of Notice is sufficiently alleged. Complaint is not uncertain and the portions of form complaint re: service of notice are sufficiently alleged. = (501/REQ)
